hermes_loop: SCHEMA: GET /v1/entities/{id}/introspection → learn property + relationship keys for this node type (cheap).
EXPAND: POST /v1/entities/{id} with targeted relationships → collect neighbor UUIDs from edges (cheap).
PIVOT: partition frontier by entity_type; for each Person/Company/Organization of interest, repeat SCHEMA→EXPAND.
OVERLAY (optional, slow ~45–55s): POST /v1/knowledge/query or /v1/knowledge/search → merge response.entities[] into frontier; each UUID is one introspection away from full structure.
REPEAT until time/token budget; treat Cala as an on-demand edge materialization API, not a single-shot answer.

steps.purpose: Resolve company name to canonical graph anchor
Discover which relationships exist before paying for retrieve payload
Materialize people + industries + investors attached to company
Person schema differs from Company — introspect before querying person edges
Expand person → other employers, CEO roles, founded companies
Walk back people linked to Arc Institute
Broad discovery: typed rows + parallel entities[] UUIDs to enqueue
steps.id_dedup_lesson: Query returned Stripe as Organization id b0154950-36cd-4d82-8473-d9119f4751c5 while entity_search returned STRIPE LLC 8a8b44f3-8769-46aa-8af8-d96c55a031b5 — same real company, different nodes; Hermes should merge by human judgment or prefer graph-consistency from one path.

steps.purpose: Pick any entity UUID from step 8 entities[] and deepen one hop (Wise example)
